hero.subtitle: NestJS Mailer + Nodemailer/SMTP, Pug templates, and an authenticated REST endpoint with inline HTML.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The email module wraps @nestjs-modules/mailer with the Pug adapter. In development, use MailHog (Docker). Use sendMail with inline html or template + context; there is also POST /email/send.

points.title: Key points
items: sendMail allows inline html (template adapter is skipped when html is present)

templates.title: Pug templates
templates.description: Add .pug files under templates/. The file name (without extension) is the template value. Use layout.pug with extends for shared HTML.
items: layout.pug — base HTML structure (optional, via extends)
context — object with variables available in Pug

config.title: SMTP configuration
config.description: smtp namespace in src/config/smtp.config.ts. Env vars:

docker.title: Docker / MailHog
docker.description: In Compose, the app points at MailHog (SMTP 1025, UI 8025). Open http://localhost:8025 to inspect messages.

service.title: EmailService
items: sendMail(options) — pass-through; use html for inline or template + context

rest.title: REST API
rest.intro: JWT required. Any authenticated user can call it (no @Roles).

send.title: Send generic email (inline HTML)
send.description: Body: to (email), subject, html. The controller uses sendMail with html — no template.

authIntegration.title: Auth integration
authIntegration.description: AuthModule imports EmailModule and uses helpers with their own templates (email-confirmation, password-reset):

usage.title: Use in another module
usage.description: Import EmailModule and inject EmailService. Inline or template:

mailhog.title: Development
mailhog.description: With MailHog, SMTP_SECURE and SMTP_REQUIRE_TLS should be false; auth can be empty.

prod.title: Production
prod.description: Use a real SMTP provider (SendGrid, SES, etc.) and strong credentials. Never commit passwords. Pug files are copied to dist via nest-cli assets.
